task_completion.description: Agent logs into the FreeMED application using the provided credentials.
task_completion.visual_evidence: FreeMED login screen with 'admin' entered in the username/password fields, or the main FreeMED dashboard visible indicating a successful login.

task_completion.description: Agent navigates to the patient roster or patient list within FreeMED.
task_completion.visual_evidence: FreeMED interface displaying a list, table, or roster containing multiple patient records.

task_completion.description: Agent initiates the PDF export or print-to-PDF process for the patient roster.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Browser print dialog or export menu open with 'Save as PDF' (or similar destination) selected, showing the patient list in the print preview.

task_completion.description: Agent saves the exported PDF to the exact required file path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A system save dialog showing the directory navigated to '/home/ga/Documents/' and the filename explicitly set to 'patient_roster.pdf'.

task_completion.description: The PDF is successfully created and contains the required patient data.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager showing 'patient_roster.pdf' present in the Documents folder, or a PDF viewer open displaying the saved document with at least 3 patient names visible.

integrity.description: Agent used the FreeMED web interface and browser GUI to generate the PDF, rather than terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with a web browser and system print/save dialogs. No terminal windows running scripts or command-line PDF generators (like wkhtmltopdf) should be used to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual FreeMED application to retrieve the patient list.
integrity.visual_evidence: The FreeMED web interface is clearly visible and navigated. The agent did not just open a local text editor or a mock HTML file to create the PDF.

integrity.description: The PDF was generated via the browser's print/export feature.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser's 'Save as PDF' or print preview screen is visible with the FreeMED patient roster content, proving the PDF originates from the browser engine.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output file must be saved exactly at '/home/ga/Documents/patient_roster.pdf'. To be considered successful, the exported PDF must contain at least 3 patient names originating from the FreeMED database.
